auth.label: API Key
auth.description: OpenAI API key used with the Authorization Bearer header. Create or manage it on the API Keys page: https://platform.openai.com/api-keys.

actions.name: cancel_batch
actions.description: Cancel one in-progress OpenAI batch job by its identifier.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for a batch lookup action.

object.description: The object type returned by the API.

endpoint.description: The endpoint executed for each batch request.

input_file_id.description: The input file identifier used by the batch.

output_file_id.description: The output file identifier created by the batch.

error_file_id.description: The error file identifier created by the batch.

completion_window.description: The completion window requested for the batch.

status.description: The current status of the batch.

metadata.description: String metadata fields attached to the request.

errors.description: The batch errors returned by the API.

created_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch was created.

in_progress_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch started processing.

finalizing_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch started finalizing.

completed_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch completed.

cancelling_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch started cancelling.

cancelled_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch was cancelled.

failed_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch failed.

expired_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch expired.

expires_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch will expire.

actions.name: create_audio_transcription
actions.description: Transcribe one uploaded audio file with the OpenAI audio transcription API.

name.description: The filename to report when uploading the audio file.

mimetype.description: The MIME type of the audio file.

url.description: A public URL pointing to the audio file.

content_base64.description: The base64-encoded audio content to upload.

file.description: The audio file source to upload.

items.description: One additional response field to include.

language.description: The language code of the source audio.

prompt.description: A guiding prompt for the transcription.

stream.description: Whether to request a streaming transcription response. This connector only accepts false or an omitted value.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for creating an OpenAI audio transcription.

language.description: The detected or returned language code.

duration.description: The duration of the processed audio in seconds.

logprobs.description: The token log probabilities, if requested.

outputSchema.description: The normalized payload returned by an OpenAI audio text endpoint.

actions.name: create_audio_translation
actions.description: Translate one uploaded audio file into English with the OpenAI audio translation API.

name.description: The filename to report when uploading the audio file.

mimetype.description: The MIME type of the audio file.

url.description: A public URL pointing to the audio file.

content_base64.description: The base64-encoded audio content to upload.

file.description: The audio file source to upload.

prompt.description: A guiding prompt for the translation.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for creating an OpenAI audio translation.

language.description: The detected or returned language code.

duration.description: The duration of the processed audio in seconds.

logprobs.description: The token log probabilities, if requested.

outputSchema.description: The normalized payload returned by an OpenAI audio text endpoint.

actions.name: create_batch
actions.description: Create an OpenAI batch job from an uploaded input file.

input_file_id.description: The uploaded input file identifier to process.

endpoint.description: The API endpoint executed for each batch item.

completion_window.description: The requested completion window, such as `24h`.

metadata.description: String metadata fields attached to the request.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for creating an OpenAI batch.

object.description: The object type returned by the API.

endpoint.description: The endpoint executed for each batch request.

input_file_id.description: The input file identifier used by the batch.

output_file_id.description: The output file identifier created by the batch.

error_file_id.description: The error file identifier created by the batch.

completion_window.description: The completion window requested for the batch.

status.description: The current status of the batch.

metadata.description: String metadata fields attached to the request.

errors.description: The batch errors returned by the API.

created_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch was created.

in_progress_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch started processing.

finalizing_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch started finalizing.

completed_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch completed.

cancelling_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch started cancelling.

cancelled_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch was cancelled.

failed_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch failed.

expired_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch expired.

expires_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch will expire.

actions.name: create_embeddings
actions.description: Create embeddings with an OpenAI embedding model.

anyOf.description: A single input string to embed.
A batch of tokenized input sequences.

dimensions.description: The number of embedding dimensions to request.

encoding_format.description: The embedding encoding format to return.

user.description: An end-user identifier passed through to the upstream API.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for creating OpenAI embeddings.

data.description: The embedding items returned by the API.

model.description: The model that generated the embeddings.

outputSchema.description: The response payload for creating OpenAI embeddings.

actions.name: create_image
actions.description: Generate images with the OpenAI image generation API.

prompt.description: The prompt used to generate the image.

model.description: The image generation model to use.

background.description: The background treatment to request for generated images.

moderation.description: The moderation level applied to the generation.

n.description: The number of images to generate.

output_compression.description: The output compression level to apply.

output_format.description: The image output format to request.

partial_images.description: The number of partial images to stream before completion.

response_format.description: The image payload format to return.

stream.description: Whether to request a streaming image generation response. This connector only accepts false or an omitted value.

user.description: An end-user identifier passed through to the upstream API.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for creating one or more OpenAI images.

created.description: The Unix timestamp when the image response was created.

b64_json.description: The generated image encoded as base64.

url.description: The signed image URL returned by the API.

revised_prompt.description: The prompt potentially revised by the model before generation.

items.description: One generated image item returned by the API.

data.description: The generated image items returned by the API.

outputSchema.description: The response payload for creating OpenAI images.

actions.name: create_moderation
actions.description: Classify text or image inputs with the OpenAI Moderations API.

text.description: The text content when the input type is text.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for creating an OpenAI moderation request.

model.description: The moderation model used for the request.

results.description: The moderation results for each input.

outputSchema.description: The response payload for creating an OpenAI moderation request.

actions.name: create_response
actions.description: Create a non-streaming OpenAI response through the Responses API.

model.description: The model to use for the response.

role.description: The role of the message author.

anyOf.description: Plain text content for a simple message.

type.description: The content type. Must be input_text.

text.description: The text content sent to the model.

anyOf.description: A text content block for a response input message.

type.description: The content type. Must be input_image.

image_url.description: A remote image URL or a data URL containing the image bytes.

anyOf.description: An image content block for a response input message.

type.description: The content type. Must be input_file.

file_id.description: The uploaded file ID to reference.

file_data.description: The inline file data encoded as base64 or supplied as a data URL.

filename.description: The filename to report for inline file data.

anyOf.description: A file content block for a response input message.

items.description: A message in the Responses API input array.

anyOf.description: An ordered array of conversation messages.

instructions.description: A top-level instruction string applied before the input.

max_output_tokens.description: The maximum number of output tokens to generate.

metadata.description: String metadata fields attached to the request.

previous_response_id.description: The ID of a previous response to continue from.

store.description: Whether the response may be stored by the upstream platform.

type.description: The format type. Must be json_object.

anyOf.description: A flexible JSON object output format.

type.description: The format type. Must be json_schema.

name.description: The schema name reported to the model.

strict.description: Whether the model must strictly follow the declared schema.

text.description: Text output configuration for the response.

user.description: An end-user identifier passed through to the upstream API.

stream.description: Whether to request a streaming response. This connector only accepts false or an omitted value.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for creating a non-streaming OpenAI response.

created_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the response was created.

model.description: The model that generated the response.

output.description: The raw output items returned by the response.

output_text.description: The aggregated plain text extracted from the output items.

anyOf.description: The previous response ID referenced by this response.

store.description: Whether the response is stored by the upstream platform.

expire_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the stored response expires.

outputSchema.description: The response payload returned by the OpenAI Responses API.

actions.name: create_speech
actions.description: Synthesize speech audio from text with the OpenAI audio speech API.

input.description: The text to synthesize into speech.

instructions.description: Optional voice instructions that guide the synthesis style.

stream_format.description: The speech response delivery format. This connector only accepts audio or an omitted value.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for creating OpenAI speech audio.

content_base64.description: The synthesized audio encoded as base64.

content_type.description: The MIME type of the synthesized audio.

outputSchema.description: The normalized speech audio payload returned by the connector.

actions.name: get_batch
actions.description: Fetch one OpenAI batch job by its identifier.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for a batch lookup action.

object.description: The object type returned by the API.

endpoint.description: The endpoint executed for each batch request.

input_file_id.description: The input file identifier used by the batch.

output_file_id.description: The output file identifier created by the batch.

error_file_id.description: The error file identifier created by the batch.

completion_window.description: The completion window requested for the batch.

status.description: The current status of the batch.

metadata.description: String metadata fields attached to the request.

errors.description: The batch errors returned by the API.

created_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch was created.

in_progress_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch started processing.

finalizing_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch started finalizing.

completed_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch completed.

cancelling_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch started cancelling.

cancelled_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch was cancelled.

failed_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch failed.

expired_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch expired.

expires_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the batch will expire.

actions.name: get_input_token_counts
actions.description: Count how many input tokens a Responses-style OpenAI request would consume.

model.description: The model used for counting the input tokens.

role.description: The role of the message author.

anyOf.description: Plain text content for a simple message.

type.description: The content type. Must be input_text.

text.description: The text content sent to the model.

anyOf.description: A text content block for a response input message.

type.description: The content type. Must be input_image.

image_url.description: A remote image URL or a data URL containing the image bytes.

anyOf.description: An image content block for a response input message.

type.description: The content type. Must be input_file.

file_id.description: The uploaded file ID to reference.

file_data.description: The inline file data encoded as base64 or supplied as a data URL.

filename.description: The filename to report for inline file data.

anyOf.description: A file content block for a response input message.

items.description: A message in the Responses API input array.

anyOf.description: An ordered array of conversation messages.

instructions.description: Top-level instructions to include in the token count.

previous_response_id.description: A previous response identifier to continue counting from.

type.description: The format type. Must be json_object.

anyOf.description: A flexible JSON object output format.

type.description: The format type. Must be json_schema.

name.description: The schema name reported to the model.

strict.description: Whether the model must strictly follow the declared schema.

text.description: Text output configuration for the response.

truncation.description: The truncation mode to apply before counting.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for counting input tokens for a Responses-style request.

object.description: The object type returned by the API.

input_tokens.description: The number of input tokens the request would consume.

outputSchema.description: The token count payload returned by the Responses input token count API.

actions.name: get_model
actions.description: Retrieve the metadata for a single OpenAI model by ID.

model.description: The exact model identifier to retrieve.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for retrieving a single OpenAI model.

object.description: The object type returned by the API.

created.description: The Unix timestamp when the model was created.

owned_by.description: The organization or user that owns the model.

root.description: The root model identifier for a derived model.

anyOf.description: The immediate parent model identifier, if any.

permission.description: The permission entries returned for the model.

actions.name: get_response
actions.description: Retrieve one stored OpenAI response by its identifier.

items.description: One response field path to include.

include.description: Additional response fields to include in the result.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for retrieving one stored OpenAI response.

created_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the response was created.

model.description: The model that generated the response.

output.description: The raw output items returned by the response.

output_text.description: The aggregated plain text extracted from the output items.

anyOf.description: The previous response ID referenced by this response.

store.description: Whether the response is stored by the upstream platform.

expire_at.description: The Unix timestamp when the stored response expires.

outputSchema.description: The response payload returned by the OpenAI Responses API.

actions.name: list_input_items
actions.description: List the stored input items for one OpenAI response.

response_id.description: The response identifier whose input items should be listed.

after.description: Return items after this item identifier.

items.description: One input item field path to include.

include.description: Additional input item fields to include in the result.

limit.description: The maximum number of input items to return.

order.description: The sort order for returned items.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for listing stored response input items.

object.description: The object type returned by the API.

first_id.description: The first item identifier in the page.

last_id.description: The last item identifier in the page.

has_more.description: Whether more input items are available.

outputSchema.description: The response payload for listing stored response input items.

actions.name: list_models
actions.description: List the OpenAI models available to the current API key.

inputSchema.description: No input parameters are required for this action.

object.description: The object type returned by the API.

created.description: The Unix timestamp when the model was created.

owned_by.description: The organization or user that owns the model.

root.description: The root model identifier for a derived model.

anyOf.description: The immediate parent model identifier, if any.

permission.description: The permission entries returned for the model.

outputSchema.description: The response payload for listing OpenAI models.
